summary: OCR-S-9, INV-OCR-7, Task 7, the schema 0.2.0 selected-Story PR-route implementation, and both unit/E2E evidence are traceable and responsibility-preserving at HEAD ce89e4589ba8c96a418249cd2f9aa673acea646b. The review cannot pass because the inspected Architecture still makes a false current-HEAD conformance claim and names an obsolete origin/main baseline.

findings.title: Refresh the Architecture conformance evidence for the reviewed HEAD/base
findings.details: Lines 143-149 claim comparison against origin/main db8f0ef3f7d3cfcb2dc0d31504e40e01025293dd and 86 current-HEAD violations (66 undeclared, 9 budget, 11 orphan). The requested review base is 6222d118315e7001abcbdc786aca932e48c58232, and `node bin/vibepro.js architecture conformance . --json` at ce89e4589ba8c96a418249cd2f9aa673acea646b reports 85 violations (65 undeclared, 9 budget, 11 orphan). Thus the document's explicit current-HEAD evidence boundary is stale even though the OCR-S-9 design itself is consistent.
findings.required_change: Recompute the origin/main baseline at 6222d118315e7001abcbdc786aca932e48c58232, update the documented baseline/current breakdown, and bind the statement to ce89e4589ba8c96a418249cd2f9aa673acea646b before recording the final spec_consistency pass.

evidence: Story Acceptance Criteria OCR-S-9 requires no undeclared probe Story and ownership derived from the selected Story PR route template.
Story Task 7 names the Architecture, Spec, test plan, execution-state owner, unit test, and Story E2E surfaces.
The invariant points back to OCR-S-9 and the Architecture section `Schema 0.2.0 reconciliation ownership`.
Its code refs resolve to managedWorktreeArtifactLocations and prRouteDirectoryIsStoryScoped in src/execution-state.js.
Its test refs exactly match the DRS-CONTRACT-009 unit case, the scenario:OCR-S-9 E2E case, and OCR-T-6 in the test plan.
managedWorktreeArtifactLocations resolves the selected Story's PR artifact and selected PR route in managed and source authorities.
prRouteDirectoryIsStoryScoped classifies the selected canonical template by a {story_id} or {feature_slug} variable in its parent directory.
The prior synthetic `${story_id}-ownership-probe` resolution is removed.
Story-scoped routes synchronize the selected directory; flat routes retain the explicit lifecycle file set.
.vibepro/qa/targeted-status-ce89e458.json is head-bound to ce89e4589ba8c96a418249cd2f9aa673acea646b and reports 124/124 pass.
.vibepro/qa/targeted-ce89e458.tap records `scenario:OCR-S-9 schema 0.2 post-merge authority sync follows the selected Story PR route` as ok 64.
.vibepro/qa/targeted-ce89e458.tap records `DRS-CONTRACT-009 schema 0.2 linked artifact sync uses catalog routing without resolving a probe Story` as ok 77.

The schema 0.2 fixtures provide matching catalog metadata and Story mirrors in managed/source authorities, use a feature_packet route with {feature_slug}, copy managed pr-merge authority to source, and commit identical execution state.
The change remains inside the existing execution-state synchronization owner.
Existing artifact-routing continues to select and resolve routes; execution-state only consumes the selected route template to classify directory ownership.
No CLI dependency, route-selection owner, copy/rollback owner, or merge authority is introduced.
Legacy flat-route and rollback coverage remain in the same targeted 124-test passing run.
